Nf3 Nc6 3. Bc4 Nf6 4. Ng5 Nxe4 |eco=[[Chess/ECOC|C57-C59]] |parent=[[Chess/Italian Game|Italian Game]] }} = Two Knights Defence = ===4...Nxe4?=== Not the best move for Black. Ignoring the threat of White's position, they grab the e-pawn. This initially seems like a good move because Black has 5...d5 after 5. Nxe4? forking the knight and bishop. However, White can now capture Black's unprotected f-pawn with check: 5. Bxf7+ Ke7 6. d4! and Black cannot yet take on g5 without losing his queen. 5. Nxf7 is a mistake, as after 5... Qh4 Black gains an advantage even against White's best defense. ==Theory table== {{ChessTable}} '''1.e4 e5 2.Nf3 Nc6 3.Bc4 Nf6 4.Ng5 Nxe4'''
